research_prompt: Research headless browsers that support SSO websites for AI agents, with output optimized for agent consumption. Evaluate: Playwright, Puppeteer, Selenium, Browserbase, browser-use, steel.dev, Stagehand, AgentQL, and any others. For each, assess SSO/auth support, agent-friendly output format, token efficiency, setup complexity, and automation reliability.

evaluation_criteria.win_condition: Each completed evaluation of a browser/tool with actionable findings is a win. Improved synthesis or comparison that adds new insight is a win.

experiments.hypothesis: Playwright is the strongest candidate due to Microsoft backing, MCP server, and accessibility tree output
experiments.reasoning: Best-in-class accessibility tree output, official MCP server, storageState for auth persistence
experiments.diff_summary: Complete Playwright evaluation covering SSO, output format, MCP integration

experiments.hypothesis: Puppeteer's direct CDP access gives advantages for advanced agent use cases
experiments.reasoning: Strong CDP access, Google MCP ecosystem, but Chrome-only and no auto-wait
experiments.diff_summary: Complete Puppeteer evaluation with CDP deep-dive and Playwright comparison

experiments.hypothesis: Selenium 4+ BiDi improvements may make it viable for agent use
experiments.reasoning: No accessibility tree API is disqualifying. BiDi helps diagnostics but doesn't close the gap
experiments.diff_summary: Complete Selenium evaluation, concluded NOT recommended for new agent work

insights: Accessibility tree is THE differentiator for agent-friendly browser tools. Playwright and Puppeteer have it; Selenium does not.
SSO auth persistence follows the same pattern everywhere: authenticate once, serialize cookies/tokens, reuse. The quality of the serialization API varies.
MFA push notifications (Microsoft Authenticator) cannot be automated by ANY tool. TOTP is the only automatable MFA method.
